description: A national referendum held on August 4, 2010, on a new Constitution of Kenya. The proposed document aimed to curb presidential powers and address land reforms; it was supported by the grand coalition government and passed overwhelmingly with 67% in favor.

key_events.description: The Church-led 'No' campaign formally opposes the draft citing abortion and land clauses.
The international community and major civil society groups endorse the 'Yes' camp.
Key government rivals Kenyatta and Odinga appear on the same platform to support the 'Yes' vote.
Peaceful voting across the country signals a historic move away from the 2007 post-election crisis.

calibration_notes: Ideal for assessing how elite-consensus-driven 'Yes' campaigns erode opposition and capture the vast majority of undecided voters in post-conflict states.
